title: Two Sum
cue: Introduces hash-map pair lookup.
traps: Using O(n^2) brute force instead of a hash map
Returning indices instead of values (or vice versa)

title: Contains Duplicate
cue: Introduces hash-set membership checks.
traps: Sorting and comparing adjacent (O(n log n)) when a set is O(n)

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
cue: Teaches single-pass running-minimum scanning.
traps: Updating min_price after computing profit instead of before
Not initializing max_profit to 0

title: Group Anagrams
cue: Covers hash-map grouping by a canonical key.
traps: Using sorted string as key (O(k log k)) when character count tuple is also valid

title: Top K Frequent Elements
cue: Covers frequency counting with ranked selection.
traps: Sorting the frequency map (O(n log n)) when a heap gives O(n log k)

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
cue: Rewards clever set usage over sorting.
traps: Sorting first (O(n log n)) instead of using a set for O(n)
Starting a sequence count at a non-start element

title: Valid Palindrome
cue: Introduces two-pointer iteration.
traps: Forgetting to skip non-alphanumeric characters
Case sensitivity issues

title: Container With Most Water
cue: Two pointers with a non-obvious pruning argument.
traps: Moving the pointer on the taller side (always move the shorter side)
Not understanding why the greedy pointer move is correct

title: 3Sum
cue: Extends two-pointer search after sorting.
traps: Not skipping duplicates after finding a valid triple
Forgetting to sort first

title: Trapping Rain Water
cue: Challenges with a hard two-pointer variant.
traps: Using O(n) space prefix/suffix arrays when O(1) two-pointer works
Off-by-one on pointer moves

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
cue: Introduces the sliding window pattern.
traps: Moving left pointer one step at a time instead of jumping to index after duplicate
Not updating char position in the map correctly

title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
cue: Extends sliding window with a replacement budget.
traps: Trying to shrink the window by updating maxCount (maxCount never needs to decrease)
Misunderstanding the shrink condition: window_size - maxCount > k

title: Minimum Window Substring
cue: Hardens sliding-window with shrink conditions.
traps: Forgetting to track how many required characters are currently satisfied
Updating result before confirming all characters are covered

title: Valid Parentheses
cue: Introduces stack-based LIFO matching.
traps: Not checking if stack is empty before popping
Not returning false when stack is non-empty at the end

title: Min Stack
cue: Designs a stack with O(1) metadata.
traps: Using a second stack of all values instead of just tracking min at each level

title: Daily Temperatures
cue: Introduces the monotonic stack.
traps: Storing temperature values instead of indices in the stack
Not computing days waited as current_index - stack_index

title: Binary Search
cue: Introduces the standard binary search template.
traps: Integer overflow on mid = (left+right)//2 (use left + (right-left)//2 in languages with overflow)
Off-by-one: left <= right vs left < right

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
cue: Applies binary search to a rotated array.
traps: Forgetting to handle the no-rotation case (arr[left] < arr[right])

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
cue: Adds a target search on top of rotation detection.
traps: Getting the two-condition logic wrong when determining which half is sorted

title: Reverse Linked List
cue: Foundational in-place pointer reversal.
traps: Losing next pointer before reassigning (save next = curr.next before overwriting)

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
cue: Builds the merge subroutine used in merge sort.
traps: Forgetting to append remaining nodes after one list is exhausted

title: Linked List Cycle
cue: Introduces Floyd's fast/slow pointer cycle detection.
traps: Not initializing slow = fast = head before the loop
Loop condition missing null checks on fast.next

title: Reorder List
cue: Combines find-middle, reverse, and merge in one problem.
traps: Not terminating the first half of the list at the middle node before reversing

title: Insert Interval
cue: Extends merge-interval logic to handle an insertion.
traps: Forgetting to append remaining intervals after the merge is done

title: Merge Intervals
cue: Core interval merge: sort then extend or append.
traps: Forgetting to sort by start time
Using strict < instead of <= for overlap condition

title: Non-overlapping Intervals
cue: Greedy interval scheduling to minimize removals.
traps: Sorting by start instead of end (greedy correctness requires sorting by end)

title: Invert Binary Tree
cue: Recursive mirror swap — introduces tree recursion.
traps: Swapping children before recursing vs after (both work, but be consistent)

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
cue: Returns 1 + max(left, right) — base case for tree DFS.
traps: Returning 0 for a non-null leaf instead of 1

title: Same Tree
cue: Parallel recursion on two trees.
traps: Checking values before checking null (always check structure first)

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
cue: BFS with level-size tracking.
traps: Iterating over the queue as it grows instead of snapshotting level size first

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
cue: Propagates min/max bounds through recursion.
traps: Only comparing node to its direct children instead of propagating bounds

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a BST
cue: Exploits BST ordering to navigate without full traversal.
traps: Using the general LCA algorithm (slower) instead of BST-specific navigation

title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
cue: Inorder traversal yields sorted order in a BST.
traps: Forgetting that inorder of BST is sorted ascending (left → root → right)

title: Number of Islands
cue: DFS/BFS flood-fill to count connected components in a grid.
traps: Not marking cells visited in-place or in a visited set before recursing
Visiting the same cell from multiple directions

title: Clone Graph
cue: BFS/DFS with a hash map to track cloned nodes.
traps: Forgetting to check if a node is already cloned before creating a new one

title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
cue: Reverse BFS from both coasts to find intersection.
traps: Doing forward flow (expensive) instead of reverse BFS from borders

title: Course Schedule
cue: Cycle detection in a directed graph (topological sort).
traps: Not handling already-visited nodes in the DFS to avoid re-processing

title: Number of Connected Components
cue: Union-Find or BFS to count disjoint sets.
traps: Forgetting to implement path compression in Union-Find

title: Word Search
cue: DFS backtracking on a 2D grid with visited marking.
traps: Not unmarking cells as unvisited when backtracking

title: Find Median from Data Stream
cue: Two heaps (max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper) maintain median.
traps: Forgetting to negate values for max-heap in Python (heapq is min-heap only)

title: Kth Largest Element in a Stream
cue: Min-heap of size k maintains the k largest elements.
traps: Using a max-heap (keeps all elements) instead of a min-heap (evicts smallest)

title: Task Scheduler
cue: Greedy scheduling with max-heap by frequency.
traps: Not using a queue/cooldown tracking mechanism for tasks on cooldown

title: Subsets
cue: Include/exclude recursion generates the power set.
traps: Appending a reference to the current list instead of a copy

title: Combination Sum
cue: Backtracking with reuse allowed (start index stays the same).
traps: Forgetting to backtrack (pop) after the recursive call

title: Word Search II
cue: Trie + DFS backtracking for multiple word search.
traps: Not pruning branches early using the Trie
Not removing found words from the Trie to avoid duplicates

title: Climbing Stairs
cue: Fibonacci recurrence — intro to bottom-up DP.
traps: Off-by-one: dp[0]=1, dp[1]=1, not dp[1]=1, dp[2]=2

title: House Robber
cue: Linear DP with skip-or-take recurrence.
traps: Not initializing dp[0] and dp[1] as base cases before the loop

title: Coin Change
cue: Unbounded knapsack — minimize coins to reach a target amount.
traps: Initializing dp with 0 instead of infinity (infinity signals 'not reachable')

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
cue: Classic O(n^2) DP or O(n log n) patience sorting.
traps: Using >= instead of > in the comparison (subsequence must be strictly increasing)

title: Word Break
cue: DP over string prefixes with word dictionary lookup.
traps: Not initializing dp[0] = True (empty string is always breakable)
